content: 2026-06-18. I'm Sam, 31, backend/ML engineer at a Paris logistics startup. Decision: stay. I negotiated +10% and a 4-day week trial starting September. Why stay: the +10% keeps my savings plan on track, and the 4-day week is the real experiment I need for life balance. Risks: startup instability, slower visible promotion, and whether the trial actually gives me the extra day off without hidden expectations. My working hypothesis: take the raise now, run the 4-day week trial for three months from September, and measure concrete results. If after the trial (review in December) productivity and compensation trajectory aren’t acceptable, I revisit moving. Need to formalize terms and get written confirmation.
Which two measurable signals will decide 'trial success' in December?

Two signals. 1) Personal: average weekly working hours drop from my current ~42 to <=32 during the trial (i.e., genuinely one full day saved each week). 2) Productivity: maintain at least 80% of current delivery throughput (tickets/merged PRs per sprint) and no uptick in critical bugs in production. Timeline: trial 1 Sept–30 Nov 2026, review meeting the first week of December 2026. Salary +10% effective immediately.
Do you want those signals and dates written into an HR/manager agreement by a firm deadline?

Yes — get it in writing. Deadline: written confirmation by 2026-06-30. I will ask for a 3-month formal trial clause: 1 Sept–30 Nov 2026, evaluation metrics as above, +10% salary effective 2026-06-01 (or pay retroactively if needed), and a review meeting scheduled for week of 2026-12-07. If outcomes fail, we agree on a remediation plan or exit terms. Action list: send email to my manager and HR today, CC product lead, propose the clause, request confirmation by 2026-06-30, and schedule the December review.
What single task do you commit to doing today to make this happen?

I will draft and send the email today. Subject: "Confirmation of +10% salary and 4-day week trial (Sept–Nov 2026)". Body: short bullets with the dates and metrics, request for written confirmation by 2026-06-30, and ask HR to add the clause to my contract or an addendum. CC HR and Thomas (manager). I will also create a 30‑minute placeholder calendar invite for the review in the week of 2026-12-07 and send it after I get agreement.
Do you want me to draft the exact email text now?

Yes — keep it factual and short. Include: the agreed +10% (state effective date), the 4-day week trial start and end dates, the two metrics (<=32h/week and >=80% throughput/no rise in critical bugs), request written confirmation by 2026-06-30, and note the review meeting in early December 2026. I'll send before 18:30 Paris time today.
Okay — I’ll draft it. Confirm send time: today at 18:30 (Paris)?
